title: Timeout Layers, Deadlines, Cancellation, Cleanup, and Budget Allocation · Go Client Systems · Go Semantics Case: Phase Budget Allocation
summary: A health aggregator has a 900 ms caller deadline spanning DNS, TLS, headers, streamed content, parsing, and cleanup. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http go phase budget allocation increment in a Go 1.26 net/http end-to-end request budget and cancellation model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Allocate an end-to-end latency budget across attempts and phases while preserving time for cleanup and fallback. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Propagate caller cancellation and deadlines through request creation, helpers, parsing, retries, and downstream work. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

theory case — Go fleet evidence model has 750 ms end-to-end for DNS, connect, TLS, headers, streamed body, parse, and cleanup. A caller cancels during body read; allocate phase budgets, propagate the reason, stop owned work, and preserve unknown remote effect.
